title: The Note on the Windshield
hook_opening: The note under Maya's wiper said 'you forgot to lock the back door.' She lived on the fourth floor.
premise: A woman finds a handwritten note that's correct about something no one should know. Introduces the recurring 'notes that predict things' motif. Ends: a second note is already waiting inside her apartment.

title: The Coworker Nobody Remembered
hook_opening: On Monday, Tom asked where Dan sat. Three people told him Dan had never worked there.
premise: A coworker someone spoke to for months is gone and erased from everyone's memory but one person's. Ends: the security log lists Tom's name on it twice.

title: The Apartment That Knew Her Schedule
hook_opening: The hallway light only came on when Priya was home. She'd never told the landlord her hours.
premise: Small wrongnesses in a new apartment imply someone is tracking the tenant's routine. Ends: she comes home early and the light is already on.

title: The Night-Shift Caller
hook_opening: Every night at 3:14, the gas station phone rang. For a week, the clerk didn't answer it.
premise: Recurring night-shift setting. The call is always the same three words. Ends: the night he finally answers, the voice uses his name.

title: The Photo He Didn't Take
hook_opening: There was a picture on Eli's phone of himself sleeping. He lived alone.
premise: Grounded paranoia about being watched inside one's own home. Ends: the timestamp is from a night he wasn't home.

title: The Woman Who Returned the Wallet
hook_opening: She handed back the wallet Jonah lost in 2019 — in a city he'd never lived in.
premise: A kind stranger who knows an impossible amount about a man's past. Ends: she says 'you dropped it the first time, too.'

title: The Door at the End of the Hall
hook_opening: Their building had fourteen apartments. The numbers went up to fifteen.
premise: A door that's always been there but no one will discuss. Liminal dread, fully grounded. Ends: one night, it's open.

title: The Texts From Her Own Number
hook_opening: Sara's phone buzzed. The message was from her, and she hadn't sent it.
premise: Texts arrive from a woman's own number, narrating what she's doing. Ends: the latest one reads 'stop reading these and turn around.'

title: The Patient in Room 6
hook_opening: Room 6 had been empty all week. The chart still updated every night at midnight.
premise: Hospital night-shift story. A discharged patient's records keep changing on their own. Ends: the newest entry lists the night nurse as next of kin.

title: The Last Note
hook_opening: The final note didn't predict anything. It just said 'thank you for keeping them.'
premise: Ties the 'notes' motif together without fully resolving it. Ends: a brand-new note appears, in Maya's own handwriting, that she doesn't remember writing.
